extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2, which requires qt_prolongation AND g6pd_deficiency AND hyperkalemia AND pregnancy. rec_drug_b is set true only by r1, which requires qt_prolongation. Whenever r2 fires, qt_prolongation is necessarily true, so r1 also fires. No other rule competes for rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b (r3/r4 only affect rec_drug_c), so priorities and defaults cannot suppress either output. With qt_prolongation=true, g6pd_deficiency=true, hyperkalemia=true, pregnancy=true (asthma/heart_failure irrelevant, set false): r1 sets rec_drug_b=true and r2 sets rec_drug_a=true, making (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) jointly true. This violates the mutual exclusion property.
